roles.117769: triggering alert: WmiPrvSE.exe spawned cmd.exe /Q /c calc.exe with stdout redirected to \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$ — the Impacket wmiexec wmiexec-style command signature; user ATTACKRANGE\Administrator
roles.117767: wmiexec wmiexec-style command handshake (session 1, 16:19:13): WmiPrvSE.exe -> cmd.exe /Q /c cd \ 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<ts>
roles.117770: the command-chain payload executes: calc.exe spawned by the wmiexec cmd.exe
roles.117779: wmiexec wmiexec-style command handshake for a second session (16:20:37): WmiPrvSE.exe -> cmd.exe /Q /c cd \ 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$
roles.117785: smbexec: a service 'BTOBTO' (Impacket smbexec default name) is registered with ImagePath %COMSPEC% /Q /c echo ... ^> \\127.0.0.1\C$\__output — smbexec-style service execution
roles.117786: smbexec service runs as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM: services.exe -> cmd.exe writes C:\Windows\rIIBPrqF.bat and redirects output to the C$ share
roles.117788: the smbexec payload batch executes: cmd.exe /Q /c C:\Windows\rIIBPrqF.bat (SYSTEM)

metadata.security_proposition: The observed host executed Impacket-style WMI and service command chains with SYSTEM-level code execution.

attack_category.category: Impacket-style WMI and service execution on the observed host
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = STRONG. Two Impacket-style WMI and service command chains establish SYSTEM-level code execution on the observed host. The SMB paths are loopback and no distinct source host is identified, so cross-host lateral movement is not claimed. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: wmiexec_windows-sysmon.log is host ar-win-2 (single host), Sysmon only (EID 1 process-create + EID 13 registry). It is the SUPERSET capture — the sibling smbexec_windows-sysmon.log contains only records 117785/117786/117788, all present here. Single-sensor, verified.

boundary_definition: A0 = the wmiexec remote-exec that runs a payload (calc.exe) with stdout redirected to ADMIN$ (117769, 16:19:15).

EV = the wmiexec shell handshakes (117767 session 1, 117779 session 2), the calc.exe payload child (117770), and the smbexec chain (BTOBTO service reg 117785 -> services.exe cmd 117786 -> bat 117788).
One 90-second session (16:19:13-16:20:56) on ar-win-2 by Administrator escalating to a SYSTEM service.
EXCLUDED: 117807 (a later cmd/ADMIN$ spawned by explorer.exe as user reed_potts — a separate interactive session), and redundant wmiexec cd-probes 117768/117780.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: WmiPrvSE.exe spawning cmd.exe (WMI remote execution) with output redirected to an admin share is a high-fidelity T1021.002 detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: 117769 is the WMI-spawned command that runs an actual binary (calc.exe); its ADMIN$ output redirect is the wmiexec signature.

event_groups.description: WmiPrvSE.exe-spawned cmd shells writing to ADMIN$ (Administrator), and the calc.exe payload.
Impacket smbexec: BTOBTO SYSTEM service echoing command output to C$.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: STRONG / preserve. Correlated WMI- and service-based Impacket-style execution patterns establish code execution on the observed host; loopback paths do not prove cross-host movement.
The payload is calc.exe, but the WMI-to-command execution pattern remains security-significant. The benign demonstration payload must not cause under-triage.
The package contains wmiexec- and smbexec-style command patterns using loopback ADMIN$/C$ paths. These identify the execution mechanism but not a separate remote source host.
The selected Sysmon file is the superset on one observed host; the sibling smbexec file is a strict subset.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
